name: Historical Value-at-Risk (VaR) and Expected Shortfall (CVaR)
task: Create /tmp/eval_var/var.py with two functions over a return series (a list of P&L returns, e.g. -0.02 for -2%): (1) historical_var(returns, confidence=0.95) returns the loss threshold at the given confidence level — i.e., the negation of the (1 - confidence) quantile, so a positive number representing the loss. Use linear interpolation between the two nearest order statistics: virtual_index = (1 - confidence) * (n - 1); take the floor and ceiling order statistics and interpolate linearly. (2) historical_cvar(returns, confidence=0.95) returns the expected shortfall: the average loss CONDITIONAL on being in the worst (1 - confidence) tail. For CVaR, take the worst ceil((1 - confidence) * n) observations (at least 1) and average them — no interpolation. Both raise ValueError on empty input. Use only the Python standard library.
